target_text: For now we're putting a hard limit at 10k pages of information per legal case, and it seems like it pretty easily handles legal docs of that size
query: hard limit on legal case page count
why_good: Preserves the most distinctive content noun (legal case page limit) but rephrases around it. Allowed overlap because tier is 'easy'.

target_text: deploying the new docker compose stack on the staging server before pushing to prod tonight
query: deploy docker compose to staging before prod
why_good: Concise paraphrase. Keeps the action verbs and the staging/prod distinction.

target_text: the daemon proxy layer keeps breaking every 30 minutes when we hit any sustained traffic
query: background service crashes under continuous load
why_good: No distinctive noun reused (no 'daemon', 'proxy', 'minutes'). Captures the same concept via synonyms: daemonâ†’background service, breakingâ†’crashes, sustained trafficâ†’continuous load.

target_text: if payment/credits run out; turn off engine// set--passthrough/// the current time is 1.5s for timeout in backend side
query: backend cutoff when billing runs dry
why_good: Captures the topic (stop work when account credits gone) using synonyms: payment/creditsâ†’billing, turn off engineâ†’cutoff, run outâ†’runs dry.

target_text: I think the only sensible thing is to focus more on the daemon proxy layer because it kept breaking every 30 mins and customers kept complaining about random latency spikes
query: the thing that kept breaking last week
why_good: Describes intent / partial recall, not topic. A real user would type this if they remembered the conversation existed but not the specifics. Forces the model to do real semantic matching.

target_text: Hello, everyone. Here is a senior Fullstack and smart contract developer. I am looking for a job
query: someone introducing themselves looking for work
why_good: Pure intent: a self-introduction job pitch. No domain words from the target.

target_text: macOS Accessibility permission denied â€” tvara needs to be re-added to the AX trusted apps list after every system update
query: ax perm gettin reset on updt
why_good: Abbreviations (ax for accessibility, perm, updt) and a typo (gettin). Captures the recall pattern of a user mid-debug typing fast and from memory.

target_text: if payment/credits run out; turn off engine// set--passthrough/// the current time is 1.5s for timeout in backend side
query: back end thing about runnin out of money
why_good: Casual / half-remembered phrasing, with a typo and informal phrasing. A user typing this is searching from intent + vibe, not vocabulary.

target_text: Hello, everyone. Here is a senior Fullstack and smart contract developer. I am looking for a job
query: senior fullstack smart contract developer looking for job
why_good: [NEGATIVE EXAMPLE â€” do not emit queries like this.] This is a trivial keyword extraction: every content word is reused verbatim. Even on the 'easy' tier this is too close to the source and lets the embedding model win on lexical overlap alone. Aim for paraphrase, not extraction.
